summary.scope_rule: Covers claim-manifest rows that are beta surface bindings, beta archetype bindings, or preview-lifecycle canonical rows governing runtime or ecosystem breadth. Held notebook-first parity is included as a downgraded-out row so notebook trust cannot be inferred as marketed beta support.

notebook_trust.summary: Benchmark publication has no notebook or kernel subject; the row still declares the absence instead of inheriting notebook trust.

repair_posture.summary: A stale public proof narrows the benchmark claim to experimental release truth instead of promoting stale performance language.

install_review.summary: No package, extension, or runtime install occurs on the public-proof row.

compatibility_label.summary: Compatibility claims are consumed through the referenced benchmark and compatibility reports, not a package label.
compatibility_label.label: not_applicable_proof_only

activation_budget.summary: Public benchmark proof stays tied to the protected benchmark budget packet and cannot widen while budget evidence is stale.

notebook_trust.summary: The TypeScript web launch wedge does not claim notebook execution; notebook trust is explicit absent truth.

repair_posture.summary: Preview launch-wedge repair remains rollback-first through generated project handoff, launch profile, and reference-workspace refresh.

install_review.summary: Workflow bundle and package install rows must pass native review before the wedge can claim beta readiness.

compatibility_label.summary: The launch wedge renders limited compatibility until the certified archetype row and compatibility report graduate.
compatibility_label.label: limited

activation_budget.summary: Launch bundle activation uses the healthy-under-budget example until live host measurements replace the seed.

notebook_trust.summary: The extension-runtime row has no notebook subject; extension webviews remain safe-preview governed.

repair_posture.summary: Crash-loop and budget repair narrows to extension quarantine, disable, or rollback without disabling the shell.

install_review.summary: Extension installs and updates must render native review, permission delta, trust, source, and rollback facts.

compatibility_label.summary: Compatibility label derives from the extension host SDK, WIT, and permission window.
compatibility_label.label: limited

activation_budget.summary: Extension activation budget is current and gates startup, warm activation, idle, and quarantine behavior.

notebook_trust.summary: Debug/test/task beta does not market notebook kernels; notebook debug stays outside this row.

repair_posture.summary: Stale or mismatched runtime evidence routes to target re-probe, launch-profile repair, or claim narrowing.

install_review.summary: This row launches existing tasks/tests/debug sessions; it does not install packages on its own.

compatibility_label.summary: Runtime compatibility is bounded by task event, command descriptor, and remote attach compatibility rows.
compatibility_label.label: limited

activation_budget.summary: Debug/test/task activation stays within runtime target and adapter budget; exhausted budget blocks auto-rerun.

notebook_trust.summary: Packaging/update/rollback does not claim notebook execution.

repair_posture.summary: Failed update repair uses exact-build rollback and state-root conflict denial before any wider mutation.

install_review.summary: Installer and updater rows render state root, handler owner, exact build, rollback target, and mutation review.

compatibility_label.summary: Desktop platform and helper compatibility labels are current but still limited by exact-build evidence.
compatibility_label.label: limited

activation_budget.summary: Installer helper activation budget remains bounded by side-by-side, rollback, and helper-startup evidence.

notebook_trust.summary: Policy/proxy/transport beta has no notebook subject and may not inherit notebook trust.

repair_posture.summary: Proxy and policy failures degrade managed lanes while preserving local editing, search, Git, and export.

install_review.summary: This row governs policy and transport state, not package install.

compatibility_label.summary: Provider, service API, browser handoff, and deployment-profile compatibility rows constrain the claim.
compatibility_label.label: limited

activation_budget.summary: Transport policy rows do not activate extension hosts; runtime budget is enforced by each downstream launch row.

notebook_trust.summary: Support export may include notebook metadata only through notebook-owned packets; this row does not claim notebook runtime.

repair_posture.summary: Support diagnostics repair preserves redaction posture and exact evidence refs before safe-mode, cache, or remote-attach recovery.

install_review.summary: Support export rows do not install or update runtime components.

compatibility_label.summary: Support export compatibility is bounded by profile layout, task event, and remote attach envelopes.
compatibility_label.label: limited

activation_budget.summary: Project Doctor and support collectors remain budgeted and may not block foreground local work.

notebook_trust.summary: Importer and migration parity does not claim notebook-first parity; notebook imports route to the held notebook row.

repair_posture.summary: Migration repair is diff-first and rollback-checkpointed; unsupported mapping narrows the row.

install_review.summary: Importing compatible extensions, settings, and workflow bundles uses native review before mutation.

compatibility_label.summary: Migration rows render exact, translated, partial, shimmed, or unsupported labels from generated compatibility truth.
compatibility_label.label: limited

activation_budget.summary: Importer rows do not activate runtime hosts until imported bundles pass their own install review.

notebook_trust.summary: Compatibility publication does not claim notebook execution and must not imply notebook-first support.

repair_posture.summary: Compatibility drift repairs by refreshing the scorecard, narrowing the row, or marking retest pending.

install_review.summary: Extension-publication and compatibility rows require install-review facts before marketplace or mirror promotion.

compatibility_label.summary: Compatibility publication reads labels from the generated compatibility report and bridge matrix.
compatibility_label.label: limited

activation_budget.summary: Extension compatibility publication carries activation-budget examples so low-cost claims cannot drift from marketplace rows.

notebook_trust.summary: The TypeScript web archetype has no notebook subject.

repair_posture.summary: Repair is limited to launch-profile, package-script, preview-route, and reference-workspace refresh.

install_review.summary: Node package, extension, and workflow-bundle setup remains native-review gated.

compatibility_label.summary: The archetype renders limited compatibility until the reference workspace report turns green.
compatibility_label.label: limited

activation_budget.summary: Package-script and preview-route activation budget is current but still preview-bound.

notebook_trust.summary: Python service/data app is notebook-adjacent only: edit, review, and export are allowed, while notebook-first parity remains held.

repair_posture.summary: Notebook-adjacent repair uses structured round-trip preview and refuses lossy metadata repair without review.

install_review.summary: Python interpreter, venv, package, and notebook-adjacent component installs require native review and rollback posture.

compatibility_label.summary: Python archetype remains limited until container/devcontainer and notebook-adjacent scorecards are current.
compatibility_label.label: limited

activation_budget.summary: Interpreter, test runner, and notebook-adjacent component activation budget is current.

notebook_trust.summary: Java/Kotlin service beta does not claim notebook execution.

repair_posture.summary: Repair is limited to build-tool, language host, and launch-profile refresh.

install_review.summary: JDK, build-tool wrapper, and extension setup must render install-review facts before mutation.

compatibility_label.summary: Java/Kotlin compatibility is limited by the archetype scorecard and version-skew rows.
compatibility_label.label: limited

activation_budget.summary: Language host and build-tool activation budget remains current and bounded.

notebook_trust.summary: Rust workspace beta does not claim notebook execution.

repair_posture.summary: Repair uses Cargo metadata refresh, launch-profile repair, and claim narrowing when reference evidence is stale.

install_review.summary: Toolchain, component, extension, and package setup render install-review facts before mutation.

compatibility_label.summary: Rust workspace compatibility is supported but still derives from the scorecard and version-skew rows.
compatibility_label.label: supported

activation_budget.summary: Rust analyzer, cargo metadata, and test-runner activation budget is current.

notebook_trust.summary: Go service or monorepo slice beta does not claim notebook execution.

repair_posture.summary: Repair is bounded to toolchain detection, module graph refresh, and launch/test profile repair.

install_review.summary: Toolchain and extension setup must render install review before mutation.

compatibility_label.summary: Go compatibility is limited by the scorecard and deployment-boundary rows.
compatibility_label.label: limited

activation_budget.summary: Go language host, module graph, and test activation budget is current.

notebook_trust.summary: C/C++ native project beta does not claim notebook execution.

repair_posture.summary: Repair is bounded to debugger/toolchain profile refresh, symbol fidelity checks, and claim narrowing.

install_review.summary: Toolchain, debugger adapter, and extension setup must render install review before mutation.

compatibility_label.summary: C/C++ compatibility is limited by the native scorecard and debug execution matrix.
compatibility_label.label: limited

activation_budget.summary: Native debugger, symbol, and language host activation budget is current.

notebook_trust.summary: Notebook-first data workflow parity is not a claimed beta row; notebook trust remains visible only for adjacent Python review flows.

repair_posture.summary: Repair packets for notebook-first parity must land before this row can be claimed.

install_review.summary: Notebook-first component install review is not a beta claim.

compatibility_label.summary: No notebook-first compatibility label is marketed in beta.
compatibility_label.label: unsupported_in_beta

activation_budget.summary: No notebook-first kernel activation budget is claimed in beta.
